chunks.en: I usually pick up a review right after my morning stand-up, when my head is still fresh.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい、朝のスタンドアップが終わってすぐ、頭がまだすっきりしているうちにレビューに取りかかります。

chunks.en: The first thing I check in the diff is whether the tests cover the new logic.
chunks.ja: 差分で最初に確認するのは、新しいロジックをテストがちゃんとカバーしているかどうかです。

chunks.en: To keep my comments from sounding harsh, I ask questions instead of giving orders.
chunks.ja: コメントがきつく聞こえないように、私は命令する代わりに質問する形にしています。

chunks.en: I approve it when the code is clear and safe, but ask for changes if something could break.
chunks.ja: コードが分かりやすくて安全なら承認しますが、何か壊れそうなときは修正をお願いします。

outline: Opening: when and how I start a review
Point 1: what I check first in the diff
Wrap-up: how I decide approve vs. request changes

modelTalk.text:
I usually pick up a review in the morning. That's when my mind is fresh. I open the pull request and read the description first. I want to know what my coworker was trying to do.

Then I look at the diff. First, I check the big changes, not the small ones. I ask myself, "Does this make sense? Is anything missing?" I also look for tests.

When I leave comments, I try to be kind. I don't say "This is wrong." I say "What do you think about this?" or "Maybe we can try this." I add a smile or a "thanks" too.

So when do I approve it? If the code works and it's easy to read, I say yes. But if something feels risky, I ask for changes. It's not personal. We just want good code together.

chunks.en: I usually pick up a pull request review right after I finish my own morning tasks.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい、自分の午前中の作業を終えた直後にプルリクエストのレビューに取りかかります。

chunks.en: The first thing I check in the diff is whether the change matches the ticket's goal.
chunks.ja: 差分で最初に確認するのは、その変更がチケットの目的に合っているかどうかです。

chunks.en: To leave comments without sounding harsh, I ask questions instead of giving orders.
chunks.ja: きつく聞こえないコメントにするために、私は命令する代わりに質問する形にしています。

chunks.en: What makes me approve it is clean code with passing tests and no big risks.
chunks.ja: 私がapproveする決め手は、テストが通っていて大きなリスクのないきれいなコードであることです。

outline: Opening: when and how I start a review
Point 1: what I check first in the diff
Wrap-up: what makes me approve or ask for changes

modelTalk.text: I usually pick up a review in the morning. My mind's fresh, so I read the code slowly. I don't jump in right after lunch. First, I check what the pull request is trying to do. I read the title and the description. Then I look at the diff. I check the main logic first, not small style stuff. I ask myself, "Does this really solve the problem?" I try to write kind comments. I use "we" instead of "you". I ask questions like, "What do you think about this?" I don't say "This is wrong." I say "Maybe we can try this." If the code works and it's clear, I just approve it. But if I'm confused, or it might break something, I ask for changes. Small things? I still approve, and leave a note. That's my way.
